evidence_text: Geoscope update covers RH, temperature, door opening times, extensometer, mini-piezometer, crackmeter, suction; laser scans with statistical interpretation were also sent.
current_use: Inventory check: these raw time series/statistical laser products are still needed before hard residuals can be formed.

evidence_text: Horizontal and vertical extensometers BCD-A9 and BCD-A10 in the closed niche failed since September 2025.
current_use: Do not treat closed-niche extensometer data after September 2025 as an unqualified calibration target.

evidence_text: Mini-piezometers BCD-A28 to BCD-A31 were reported as working well.
current_use: Good candidates for future pressure residuals once the Geoscope numeric time series are located.

evidence_text: Crackmeter data indicate an ongoing trend at one closed-niche measuring point and seasonal variation in the open niche.
current_use: Use as a qualitative deformation sanity check until crackmeter series are collected.

evidence_text: Largest levelling deformations are CDA-O1 -2.1 mm settlement and CDA-C4 +1.3 mm heave/uplift; most other points tend toward uplift.
current_use: Numeric levelling rows are extracted separately; use as deformation validation after matching the survey reference frame to OGS displacement output.

evidence_text: BGR-EDF comparison focus is displacements/strains from extensometers, pore pressure from mini-piezometers, and pore pressure in the desaturated zone from suction.
current_use: Defines the priority order for future HM observation operators beyond permeability, NMR, ERT, Taupe and RH.

evidence_text: BGR reports access to long-term deformation, humidity, water content, pore-water pressure, crack-width, piezometer/extensometer, ERT, TDR and laser-scan measurements.
current_use: Confirms that the catalogue should keep seeking Geoscope and laser-scan raw exports, not just the water-content and permeability files already normalized.

evidence_text: Slide-summary table has 12 points; full campaign table with covariance/reference-frame metadata is still preferred.
